before.description: Skipped heading levels, multiple h1 elements, inconsistent hierarchy

after.description: Proper hierarchy, single h1, logical nesting

before.description: Using only visual formatting tags - no semantic meaning

after.description: Semantic elements convey meaning to assistive technologies

before.code:
Step 1: Do this

Step 2: Do that

Step 3: Finish

- Item 1
- Item 2
- Item 3
before.description: Using paragraphs and line breaks instead of proper list elements

after.description: Proper list elements with clear structure and meaning

before.code:
"This is a quote" - Author

This is an important quote from a book.

after.code:
This is an important quote from a book.

Author Name

insights: Always use semantic elements (strong, em) instead of visual-only tags (b, i)
Maintain proper heading hierarchy - never skip levels
Use lists (ol, ul, dl) for list content, not paragraphs with line breaks
Use blockquote for extended quotations, not div with manual styling
One h1 per page, followed by h2 for sections, h3 for subsections
